posts.body:
As you may already know there are two separate character boards for vagabond, one of them requires black pawn, and another one requires white pawn. One of these boards contains quest cards as faction components, another one doesn't. Also I have all available vagabond cards with pawns. Each pawn corelate exactly to one vagabond card (by art)
So here is my question. 
- Are those character boards completely interchangeable?
- May I use any white color pawns correlated to a specific vagabond card, if my character board requires a black pawn?
- Can I pick any of the vagabond boards if there is only one vagabond in the game?
- Are there quests in the game if I choose to play black vagabond without white vagabond?

- Yes.
- Yes. The color of the pawns is irrelevant.
- Yes. Both Vagabonds are similar.
- No. Vagabonds share the Quest deck, and playing with any pawn is irrelevant for the purpose of playing (other than aesthetics).
Thank you for replying, but what then is the purpose of specifying black and white pawns if it doesn't matter?

Originally, there was only one Vagabond. The Riverfolk expansion (2018) allows to play with a second Vagabond (that is, two Vagabonds in the same session), and in that case you needed to differentiate them, hence a black meeple and a grey meeple; there was never a need to match the card with the meeple. When The Vagabond Pack (2020, two years later) was published, now you could match the Vagabond you chose with the particular meeple, but that's that, aesthetics.

Thank you very much.
I understand it now. I forgot that initially it was literally the same pawn in different colors, and then the vagabond pack came in.

You can use whichever board you want with whichever meeple you want. Matching the board with the meeple was never a thing. (Personally, I didn’t even know the boards could be differentiated in any way.)

Usually you’ll want to match the meeple with the card, since each meeple actually corresponds to one card, but even that is not necessary. As long as you play by your card, you can totally use any meeple you want to represent that card.  

(Unless you’re playing a 2-vagabond game, in which case it would probably be confusing for everyone if either Vagabond’s meeple didn’t match their card. But you probably shouldn’t be playing 2-vagabond games anyway.)
